questions.question: What is the time complexity of searching for an element in a binary search tree?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of searching for an element in a binary search tree is O(log n) because the search space is reduced by half at each level.

questions.question: Which of the following operations is not possible in a binary tree?
questions.explanation: Sorting is not possible in a binary tree as it does not have a fixed order.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the in-order traversal in a binary tree?
options: To traverse the tree in ascending order
To traverse the tree in descending order
questions.explanation: The in-order traversal visits the left subtree, the current node, and then the right subtree, resulting in a tree traversal in ascending order.

questions.question: Which of the following data types can be used to represent a binary tree?
questions.explanation: A stack can be used to represent a binary tree by using the top of the stack to keep track of the current node.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the height of a binary tree?
options: To measure the number of nodes in the tree
To measure the number of edges in the tree
To measure the number of levels in the tree
To measure the number of nodes at each level
questions.explanation: The height of a binary tree is the number of edges from the root node to the furthest leaf node, which can be used to measure the number of levels in the tree.
